profile.markdown:
"King of the Fire Demons."

A warrior who believed in the Abyss and was appointed as the leader of the Abyss Legion. Because the Fire Demons were affected by the Abyss for thousands of years and are considered the race closest to Abyss, Thamuz has always had a high sense of superiority and self-esteem.

- Chinese name: 炎煞 (yánshā, "Flaming Demon")
- Alias: Lord Lava; The Annihilator (Nexus Sea); The Great Scourge (Nexus Sea)
- Species: Fire demon
- Gender: Male
- Title: King of Fire Demons
- Affiliation: The Abyss; Moniyan Empire (hostile); Moon elves (hostile)
- Weapons: Molten Scythes
- Abilities:
  - Conjures a burst of Lava Energy by chance
  - Spouts lava restored in his body and creates a cauterant atmosphere around him

story.markdown:
Deep within the mountains, darkness reigns over everything. The only light is the crimson lava that flows in the bottomless crevices. Since the beginning of the abyss's reign on the mainland, countless demons became loyal followers of the Abyss. Fervent in their beliefs, the demons chant the will of the Abyss in these deep caverns—Only the strong deserve to rule!

Guarding the Abyss for millenniums, a tribe of fire demons nearly became the rulers of the Abyss. Thamuz, the king of the fire demons, and his people made the Abyss into a formidable power, unabashed to use cruel methods in achieving his goals. Possessing ungodly natural strength, Thamuz was appointed chief of the Abyss, who led the Abyss to conquer all of the underworld.

To Thamuz, all of his opponents were worthless scum, unworthy of living in this world. In his eyes, only he was worthy to leading. Thamuz could not accept the Blood Demon, Alice, as her bloodline was not pure — Thus, unworthy to command and enter war.

With the gates to the Abyss about to open, legions of demons eagerly await Thamuz's return. However, once the King of Fire Demons returns, he will burn away all the weak demons — Only the strong deserve to be a part of the Abyssal Army. Thamuz has been waiting for the day he leads the Abyssal Army across the Land of Dawn to let it be known—Only the strong shall reign!

content.markdown:
In the Determination event, Thamuz claims himself as the Lord of Darkness and wants Lunox to join him.

Thamuz appears in Helcurt's lore, wherein when Helcurt was tasked to defend the Sovereign of the Abyss, Obsidia, the opposing forces marching towards her palace were led by none other than the Lord Lava himself.

In Dyrroth's trailer, Thamuz praises Dyrroth, seeing a part of himself in the demon prince, mentioning that he "inherited his brutality and valor". Dyrroth also mentions Thamuz in one of his voicelines, mocking how his enemy should've been taught by Thamuz instead, praising his own technique.

Thamuz is also half-heartedly mentioned in two of Helcurt's voicelines, describing him to be a "walking puddle of lava with a pair of dull blades", whilst also mockingly calling out to him. Their relationship could be attributed to that of a father and a family pet.

Thamuz seems to show genuine empathy towards his kind, depicting him as a strict yet well-meaning ruler, prioritizing his tribe's survival over all else as shown in multiple artworks posted on official accounts.

A silhouette that looks very similar to Thamuz appears in Nolan's cinematic, A New Journey, wherein he talks about the origins of the Light and Darkness and the Era of Strife. Considering that the events are implied to have taken place an unknown amount of time in the past, it could be theorized that Thamuz has been fighting for the Abyss for longer than assumed.

During the Abyss's invasion of the Moonlit Forest (in "Belerick Sacrificed Himself! Who's Responsible?"), Thamuz indirectly killed Belerick, as he sacrificed his life to defend the Tree of Life. When Belerick was revived, his skillset was revamped to better counter continuously damaging opponents. One such enemy, being Thamuz.

Whether or not Belerick grew specifically to defend against him is unclear. However, there have been multiple real-life cases of plants evolving and adapting to specific threats.

In the official comic "Demons Approaching" debuting Brody, Benedetta, and Phoveus, Thamuz reveals that he is in possession of one of the "Nine Demon Lords of the Abyss", an orange crystal that Astaros recognizes as "Gamunti". Due to Phoveus's revamped lore however, it is unclear on whether or not this remains canon.

In the cinematic Sovereign's Will, Thamuz interrupts Alice and Dyrroth from harvesting Obsidia's blood. Convinced that her corruption still lives within her bloodstream, he expresses his frustration as a result of Obsidia's past crimes against his people, corrupting them into Spawns of Decay.

Shortly after, Thamuz encounters a corrupted Fire Demon, hesitating, before being forced to kill it, fueling his rage further. Due to the small stature of the Fire Demon, it is possible that Thamuz was forced to kill a juvenile member of his species, making it the second depiction of a youth's death, the first being Mila, in Arlott's lore.

In Obsidia's concept video, Thamuz's race, the Fire Demons, do not seem to naturally grow a head as he does when compared to the usual depictions of his kind. This might suggest that Thamuz may have carved it himself to assert his dominance as the rightful Lord Lava.

This is further reinforced by the entry in Chapter 1 of his Mastery Code, where Thamuz is described to have "forged his body from lava".

In the seasonal story for Season 38, Obsidia encounters a Fire Demon corrupted by the God of Darkness and on the verge of turning into a Spawn of Decay, breaking down as he fears for not only for himself, but for his tribesmen. The fire demon, in his last moments, isolates himself from the rest of his people to protect them from disease, in the name of Lord Thamuz.

The Annihilator is heavily based off of H.P. Lovecraft and his works of cosmic horror, primarily of the Cthulhu mythos, wherein beings commonly known as the "Outer Gods" (a term popularized by the heavily Lovecraft inspired roleplaying game "Call of Cthulhu") exist beyond human comprehension.

They are described to serve purposes far beyond mortal understanding, and are known by many names (in Thamuz's case, he's also known as "The Great Scourge"), very similar to the skin's accompanying lore. Additionally, a squid-like buckle adorned with wings can be seen on his model, heavily reminiscent of Cthulhu's (the titular Outer God) most common depiction.

Thamuz's original hero showcase video on the official YouTube channel had an abrupt change in thumbnail design. Thamuz had four different walking animations for his Scytheless form throughout his development. In the advanced server prior to his release, Thamuz used to run on all fours without his scythes. On release, he instead walked on his hind legs, slightly leaned in with his arms stretched outward.

On his soft rework on Patch 1.6.84 on May 24, 2022, his posture was changed to be straighter and started to jog, more akin to how a human person would run. However, this change would soon be rolled back to the previous version an unknown amount of updates later. On his revamp on September 17, 2025, he was given a new scytheless animation, similar to his removed one on Patch 1.6.84.

Thamuz was the first hero introduced from the Abyss faction, predating heroes like Alice, Dyrroth, and Selena in the lore. His passive Grand Lord Lava was one of the first abilities in the game to consistently deal true damage based on enemies' HP.

Some players compare his look to Balrog from Lord of the Rings or Ifrit from Final Fantasy. His weapons, Molten Scythes, return to him on their own, similar to boomerangs. After his revamp, Thamuz now has different glow effects on his model during his ultimate, depending on his skin. When Thamuz moves, a lava trail forms behind him, showing his burning presence. He is one of the few heroes whose footsteps leave elemental effects on the battlefield. Unfortunately, this has been since removed after his revamp.

details.markdown:
Thamuz's basic attacks have a 10%-60% chance (based on level or stats) to conjure Lava Energy beneath the target that erupts after a brief delay, dealing scaling damage as skill damage. Basic attacks that don't trigger Lava Energy will increase Thamuz's Lava Rage.

When without his scythes, Thamuz gains 25% Movement Speed. After retrieving his scythes, his next basic attack slows the target by 30% and is guaranteed to trigger Lava Energy. The enhanced basic attack allows Thamuz to dash to the target's location, with an approximate range of 3.0.

Notes:
- Lava Energy deals skill damage.
- The enhanced basic attack allows Thamuz to dash to the target's location.
- The enhanced basic attack range is 3.0.

Thamuz tosses his scythes in the target direction. After travelling for a certain distance or hitting the first enemy hero, they deal base physical damage scaling with level and physical attack. The scythes will then slow nearby enemies by 30% and deal sustained physical damage every 0.5 seconds.

After leaving Thamuz for around 5 seconds or a certain distance, the scythes will return to Thamuz and drag enemies in their path to him. Thamuz can approach the scythes or cast the skill again to retrieve them.

Level scaling:
- Base Damage: 120 / 150 / 180 / 210 / 240 / 270
- Sustained Damage: 45 / 52 / 59 / 66 / 73 / 80

Notes:
- Cooldown starts after retrieving the scythes.
- The scythes do not stop upon hitting minions (added on Patch Notes 1.6.84).
- Thamuz cannot cast the 2nd phase of Molten Scythes for approximately 0.2 seconds after casting the same skill.
- Retrieving the scythes by tapping again the skill is not considered as a skill cast.
- Thamuz can use this skill while under crowd control effects, including Suppression.
- Unlike his passive, the enhanced basic attack gained from retrieving the scythes is considered a movement ability, meaning Phoveus can benefit from it.

Thamuz jumps to a designated area to deal physical damage in an area and slow the enemy units by 25% for 2 seconds upon landing. If the scythes are still travelling, they will return to Thamuz and Molten Scythes will reset its cooldown instantly.

Level scaling:
- Base Damage: 230 / 255 / 280 / 305 / 330 / 355

Notes:
- Chasm Trample follows the same logic as certain jump abilities such as Guinevere's, meaning it can reach a longer distance when used at a maximum distance behind a wall.

Thamuz spouts the lava stored in his body, dealing an initial burst of physical damage to nearby enemies and creating a cauterant inferno around him. The Cauterant Inferno lasts 9 seconds and deals sustained physical damage every 0.5 seconds to enemies in the area.

Meanwhile, Thamuz gains extra Max HP, heals himself by the same amount, and increases his Attack Speed by 60%-100% depending on skill level. He also recovers 1.5%-2.5% Max HP each time he deals damage with basic attacks or his skills during the inferno.

Level scaling:
- Base Damage: 300 / 390 / 480
- Sustained Damage: 30 / 45 / 60
- HP Regen on hit: 1.5% / 2% / 2.5% Max HP
- Attack Speed: 60% / 80% / 100%
- Max HP increase: 30% / 40% / 50%

Notes:
- The attack speed increase is multiplied to the total attack speed.
- Healing during dealing damage with skills and basic attacks does not include sustained damage from skills and effects.

Thamuz is mocked in Helcurt's voicelines as a walking puddle of lava with a pair of dull blades, hinting at a pet-like dynamic between them.
A silhouette similar to Thamuz appears in Nolan's cinematic A New Journey, suggesting he has fought for the Abyss since the distant past.
During the Abyss's invasion of the Moonlit Forest, Thamuz indirectly killed Belerick, who sacrificed himself to defend the Tree of Life.
In the comic Demons Approaching, Thamuz holds an orange crystal called Gamunti, one of the Nine Demon Lords of the Abyss, though its canon status is unclear.
In Sovereign's Will, Thamuz attacks Alice and Dyrroth for harvesting Obsidia's blood and kills a corrupted juvenile Fire Demon, fueling his rage.
Thamuz may have forged his own head from lava to assert dominance, as Fire Demons typically lack such a head in other depictions.
The Annihilator skin for Thamuz draws heavy inspiration from H.P. Lovecraft's Cthulhu mythos and cosmic horror, including Cthulhu-like visual elements.
Thamuz was the first hero introduced from the Abyss faction in lore, predating Alice, Dyrroth, and Selena.
His passive Grand Lord Lava was among the first abilities to consistently deal true damage based on enemies' HP.
Some players compare Thamuz's appearance to Balrog from Lord of the Rings or Ifrit from Final Fantasy.
Thamuz has had multiple different walking animations without his scythes across patches, including running on all fours and various upright stances.
After his revamp, Thamuz gained different glow effects during his ultimate depending on his skin, and at one point left a lava trail when moving, later removed.

markdown:
# Profile

"King of the Fire Demons."

A warrior who believed in the Abyss and was appointed as the leader of the Abyss Legion. Because the Fire Demons were affected by the Abyss for thousands of years and are considered the race closest to Abyss, Thamuz has always had a high sense of superiority and self-esteem.

- Chinese name: 炎煞 (yánshā, "Flaming Demon")
- Alias: Lord Lava; The Annihilator (Nexus Sea); The Great Scourge (Nexus Sea)
- Species: Fire demon
- Gender: Male
- Title: King of Fire Demons
- Affiliation: The Abyss; Moniyan Empire (hostile); Moon elves (hostile)
- Weapons: Molten Scythes
- Abilities:
  - Conjures a burst of Lava Energy by chance
  - Spouts lava restored in his body and creates a cauterant atmosphere around him

# Story

Deep within the mountains, darkness reigns over everything. The only light is the crimson lava that flows in the bottomless crevices. Since the beginning of the abyss's reign on the mainland, countless demons became loyal followers of the Abyss. Fervent in their beliefs, the demons chant the will of the Abyss in these deep caverns—Only the strong deserve to rule!

Guarding the Abyss for millenniums, a tribe of fire demons nearly became the rulers of the Abyss. Thamuz, the king of the fire demons, and his people made the Abyss into a formidable power, unabashed to use cruel methods in achieving his goals. Possessing ungodly natural strength, Thamuz was appointed chief of the Abyss, who led the Abyss to conquer all of the underworld.

With the gates to the Abyss about to open, legions of demons eagerly await Thamuz's return. However, once the King of Fire Demons returns, he will burn away all the weak demons — Only the strong deserve to be a part of the Abyssal Army. Thamuz has been waiting for the day he leads the Abyssal Army across the Land of Dawn to let it be known—Only the strong shall reign!

# Side Story

## Trivia and Connections

In the Determination event, Thamuz claims himself as the Lord of Darkness and wants Lunox to join him.

Thamuz appears in Helcurt's lore, wherein when Helcurt was tasked to defend the Sovereign of the Abyss, Obsidia, the opposing forces marching towards her palace were led by none other than the Lord Lava himself.

In Dyrroth's trailer, Thamuz praises Dyrroth, seeing a part of himself in the demon prince, mentioning that he "inherited his brutality and valor". Dyrroth also mentions Thamuz in one of his voicelines, mocking how his enemy should've been taught by Thamuz instead, praising his own technique.

Thamuz is also half-heartedly mentioned in two of Helcurt's voicelines, describing him to be a "walking puddle of lava with a pair of dull blades", whilst also mockingly calling out to him. Their relationship could be attributed to that of a father and a family pet.

Thamuz seems to show genuine empathy towards his kind, depicting him as a strict yet well-meaning ruler, prioritizing his tribe's survival over all else as shown in multiple artworks posted on official accounts.

A silhouette that looks very similar to Thamuz appears in Nolan's cinematic, A New Journey, wherein he talks about the origins of the Light and Darkness and the Era of Strife. Considering that the events are implied to have taken place an unknown amount of time in the past, it could be theorized that Thamuz has been fighting for the Abyss for longer than assumed.

## Belerick Sacrificed Himself

During the Abyss's invasion of the Moonlit Forest (in "Belerick Sacrificed Himself! Who's Responsible?"), Thamuz indirectly killed Belerick, as he sacrificed his life to defend the Tree of Life. When Belerick was revived, his skillset was revamped to better counter continuously damaging opponents. One such enemy, being Thamuz.

Whether or not Belerick grew specifically to defend against him is unclear. However, there have been multiple real-life cases of plants evolving and adapting to specific threats.

## Demons Approaching

In the official comic "Demons Approaching" debuting Brody, Benedetta, and Phoveus, Thamuz reveals that he is in possession of one of the "Nine Demon Lords of the Abyss", an orange crystal that Astaros recognizes as "Gamunti". Due to Phoveus's revamped lore however, it is unclear on whether or not this remains canon.

## Sovereign's Will and Fire Demons

In the cinematic Sovereign's Will, Thamuz interrupts Alice and Dyrroth from harvesting Obsidia's blood. Convinced that her corruption still lives within her bloodstream, he expresses his frustration as a result of Obsidia's past crimes against his people, corrupting them into Spawns of Decay.

Shortly after, Thamuz encounters a corrupted Fire Demon, hesitating, before being forced to kill it, fueling his rage further. Due to the small stature of the Fire Demon, it is possible that Thamuz was forced to kill a juvenile member of his species, making it the second depiction of a youth's death, the first being Mila, in Arlott's lore.

In Obsidia's concept video, Thamuz's race, the Fire Demons, do not seem to naturally grow a head as he does when compared to the usual depictions of his kind. This might suggest that Thamuz may have carved it himself to assert his dominance as the rightful Lord Lava.

This is further reinforced by the entry in Chapter 1 of his Mastery Code, where Thamuz is described to have "forged his body from lava".

In the seasonal story for Season 38, Obsidia encounters a Fire Demon corrupted by the God of Darkness and on the verge of turning into a Spawn of Decay, breaking down as he fears for not only for himself, but for his tribesmen. The fire demon, in his last moments, isolates himself from the rest of his people to protect them from disease, in the name of Lord Thamuz.

## Nexus Sea Inspiration

The Annihilator is heavily based off of H.P. Lovecraft and his works of cosmic horror, primarily of the Cthulhu mythos, wherein beings commonly known as the "Outer Gods" (a term popularized by the heavily Lovecraft inspired roleplaying game "Call of Cthulhu") exist beyond human comprehension.

They are described to serve purposes far beyond mortal understanding, and are known by many names (in Thamuz's case, he's also known as "The Great Scourge"), very similar to the skin's accompanying lore. Additionally, a squid-like buckle adorned with wings can be seen on his model, heavily reminiscent of Cthulhu's (the titular Outer God) most common depiction.

## Miscellaneous Notes

Thamuz's original hero showcase video on the official YouTube channel had an abrupt change in thumbnail design. Thamuz had four different walking animations for his Scytheless form throughout his development. In the advanced server prior to his release, Thamuz used to run on all fours without his scythes. On release, he instead walked on his hind legs, slightly leaned in with his arms stretched outward.

On his soft rework on Patch 1.6.84 on May 24, 2022, his posture was changed to be straighter and started to jog, more akin to how a human person would run. However, this change would soon be rolled back to the previous version an unknown amount of updates later. On his revamp on September 17, 2025, he was given a new scytheless animation, similar to his removed one on Patch 1.6.84.

Thamuz was the first hero introduced from the Abyss faction, predating heroes like Alice, Dyrroth, and Selena in the lore. His passive Grand Lord Lava was one of the first abilities in the game to consistently deal true damage based on enemies' HP.

Some players compare his look to Balrog from Lord of the Rings or Ifrit from Final Fantasy. His weapons, Molten Scythes, return to him on their own, similar to boomerangs. After his revamp, Thamuz now has different glow effects on his model during his ultimate, depending on his skin. When Thamuz moves, a lava trail forms behind him, showing his burning presence. He is one of the few heroes whose footsteps leave elemental effects on the battlefield. Unfortunately, this has been since removed after his revamp.

# Abilities

## Grand Lord Lava

Thamuz's basic attacks have a 10%-60% chance (based on level or stats) to conjure Lava Energy beneath the target that erupts after a brief delay, dealing scaling damage as skill damage. Basic attacks that don't trigger Lava Energy will increase Thamuz's Lava Rage.

When without his scythes, Thamuz gains 25% Movement Speed. After retrieving his scythes, his next basic attack slows the target by 30% and is guaranteed to trigger Lava Energy. The enhanced basic attack allows Thamuz to dash to the target's location, with an approximate range of 3.0.

Notes:
- Lava Energy deals skill damage.
- The enhanced basic attack allows Thamuz to dash to the target's location.
- The enhanced basic attack range is 3.0.

## Molten Scythes

Thamuz tosses his scythes in the target direction. After travelling for a certain distance or hitting the first enemy hero, they deal base physical damage scaling with level and physical attack. The scythes will then slow nearby enemies by 30% and deal sustained physical damage every 0.5 seconds.

After leaving Thamuz for around 5 seconds or a certain distance, the scythes will return to Thamuz and drag enemies in their path to him. Thamuz can approach the scythes or cast the skill again to retrieve them.

Level scaling:
- Base Damage: 120 / 150 / 180 / 210 / 240 / 270
- Sustained Damage: 45 / 52 / 59 / 66 / 73 / 80

Notes:
- Cooldown starts after retrieving the scythes.
- The scythes do not stop upon hitting minions (added on Patch Notes 1.6.84).
- Thamuz cannot cast the 2nd phase of Molten Scythes for approximately 0.2 seconds after casting the same skill.
- Retrieving the scythes by tapping again the skill is not considered as a skill cast.
- Thamuz can use this skill while under crowd control effects, including Suppression.
- Unlike his passive, the enhanced basic attack gained from retrieving the scythes is considered a movement ability, meaning Phoveus can benefit from it.

## Chasm Trample

Thamuz jumps to a designated area to deal physical damage in an area and slow the enemy units by 25% for 2 seconds upon landing. If the scythes are still travelling, they will return to Thamuz and Molten Scythes will reset its cooldown instantly.

Level scaling:
- Base Damage: 230 / 255 / 280 / 305 / 330 / 355

Notes:
- Chasm Trample follows the same logic as certain jump abilities such as Guinevere's, meaning it can reach a longer distance when used at a maximum distance behind a wall.

## Cauterant Inferno

Thamuz spouts the lava stored in his body, dealing an initial burst of physical damage to nearby enemies and creating a cauterant inferno around him. The Cauterant Inferno lasts 9 seconds and deals sustained physical damage every 0.5 seconds to enemies in the area.

Meanwhile, Thamuz gains extra Max HP, heals himself by the same amount, and increases his Attack Speed by 60%-100% depending on skill level. He also recovers 1.5%-2.5% Max HP each time he deals damage with basic attacks or his skills during the inferno.

Level scaling:
- Base Damage: 300 / 390 / 480
- Sustained Damage: 30 / 45 / 60
- HP Regen on hit: 1.5% / 2% / 2.5% Max HP
- Attack Speed: 60% / 80% / 100%
- Max HP increase: 30% / 40% / 50%

Notes:
- The attack speed increase is multiplied to the total attack speed.
- Healing during dealing damage with skills and basic attacks does not include sustained damage from skills and effects.

# Trivia

- In the Determination event, Thamuz claims himself as the Lord of Darkness and wants Lunox to join him.
- Thamuz appears in Helcurt's lore, when Helcurt was tasked to defend the Sovereign of the Abyss, Obsidia, against forces led by Thamuz.
- In Dyrroth's trailer, Thamuz praises Dyrroth, saying he inherited his brutality and valor; Dyrroth also mentions Thamuz in his voicelines.
- Thamuz is mocked in Helcurt's voicelines as a walking puddle of lava with a pair of dull blades, hinting at a pet-like dynamic between them.
- A silhouette similar to Thamuz appears in Nolan's cinematic A New Journey, suggesting he has fought for the Abyss since the distant past.
- During the Abyss's invasion of the Moonlit Forest, Thamuz indirectly killed Belerick, who sacrificed himself to defend the Tree of Life.
- In the comic Demons Approaching, Thamuz holds an orange crystal called Gamunti, one of the Nine Demon Lords of the Abyss, though its canon status is unclear.
- In Sovereign's Will, Thamuz attacks Alice and Dyrroth for harvesting Obsidia's blood and kills a corrupted juvenile Fire Demon, fueling his rage.
- Thamuz may have forged his own head from lava to assert dominance, as Fire Demons typically lack such a head in other depictions.
- The Annihilator skin for Thamuz draws heavy inspiration from H.P. Lovecraft's Cthulhu mythos and cosmic horror, including Cthulhu-like visual elements.
- Thamuz was the first hero introduced from the Abyss faction in lore, predating Alice, Dyrroth, and Selena.
- His passive Grand Lord Lava was among the first abilities to consistently deal true damage based on enemies' HP.
- Some players compare Thamuz's appearance to Balrog from Lord of the Rings or Ifrit from Final Fantasy.
- Thamuz has had multiple different walking animations without his scythes across patches, including running on all fours and various upright stances.
- After his revamp, Thamuz gained different glow effects during his ultimate depending on his skin, and at one point left a lava trail when moving, later removed.

metadata.hook: With lava-forged flesh and an iron creed that only the strong deserve to rule, Thamuz waits to march the Abyssal Army across the Land of Dawn.
metadata.teaser: Deep beneath the mountains, the King of Fire Demons sharpens his lava-forged scythes, ready to purge the weak and lead the Abyss against the Land of Dawn.
metadata.openingLine: Deep within the mountains, darkness reigns over everything.
plotKeywords: only the strong deserve to rule
